BASE.NO_TOURS_OR_WORKSHOPS: No tours or workshops scheduled for this day.
BASE.LABEL: Capacity
BASE.WHO_WE_ARE_PEREX: Behind every product from Modra stands a person — their hands, experience, and sense of detail.
BASE.WHO_WE_ARE_PEREX2: The team of Slovak Folk Majolica consists of masters, artistic painters, craftsmen, lecturers, and management staff who together keep the tradition of Modra majolica alive and move it forward. Each of us contributes to ensuring that majolica is created with the same respect as it was by generations before us, and that it continues to have its place in today's world.

feedback.login_required: Login required to book a session.

HOME.PEREX: Modra majolica and majolica ornamentation are inscribed in the Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Slovakia.
HOME.FIRST_TEXT: For 140 years, masters of the craft in Slovak Folk Majolica have been developing inherited traditions, creating the ceramic soul of the region with their hands and hearts. All products are handmade and hand-painted. Each piece is a unique work of art. Modra Majolica and Majolica ornamentation are listed in the Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage of Slovakia.

ONAS.MAJOLIKA_DOMA_TEXT:
Slovak folk majolica is one of the bearers of the tradition of Modra majolica and majolica ornamentation, while also serving as a space for creativity and innovative approaches in the production of Modra ceramics. The aim of the project support is to develop creative talent and innovation in the field of traditional crafts through the preservation and restoration of an important part of Slovakia’s cultural heritage.

Discover unique hand-painted vases from the Majolika workshop. A perfect addition to your interior.

ESHOP.FIRST_TEXT: Slovak Folk Majolica is a bearer of the tradition of Modra Majolica and Majolica ornamentation, while also being a space for creativity and innovative approaches in the production of Modra ceramics. The goal of project support is to develop creative talent and innovations in the field of traditional crafts by preserving, maintaining, and restoring a significant part of Slovakia’s cultural heritage.

DIELNE.FIRST_TEXT: Discover the ancient beauty of traditional folk craftsmanship. We offer you a unique opportunity to peek behind the scenes of the largest craft workshops with the longest tradition in Slovakia. Modra Majolica and Majolica ornamentation are listed in the Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage of Slovakia.

TRADICIA.FIRST_TEXT: In 1883, the Ceramic Industrial School (Agyagipar Tanműhely Modor) was founded in Modra. The establishment of this school laid the foundation for the future prosperous Slovak Ceramics company and later the Slovak Folk Majolica, which has continuously operated up to the present day. The school was founded by the town of Modra in cooperation with the Hungarian Ministry of Industry and Trade. The first director was Jozef Mička, a ceramist from Moravia. Apprentices learned the most important workshop skills, such as pottery, painting, and firing.

ABOUT.TITLE: The Majolika Story
ABOUT.TEXT: At Slovenská ľudová majolika, master artisans have been nurturing inherited traditions for 140 years, shaping the ceramic soul of the region with their hands and hearts. All products are handmade and hand-painted. Every piece is a unique work of art. Modra majolika and majolica ornamentation are inscribed on the Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Slovakia. Click to learn more
